title: avena secalina
intro: Avena secalina, also known as wild oats, is a wild grass species closely related to the cultivated oats. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea, and skin problems, including eczema and acne. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which provide several health benefits, including soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. Avena secalina is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and can also be made into a tincture or poultice for topical application.
